title: Zomato, Blinkit roll out heatwave support measures for delivery partners - Storyboard18

full_text:
Amid an intense heatwave across India, Zomato and Blinkit, both part of the Eternal group, have announced a series of measures aimed at supporting delivery partners operating in extreme weather conditions, according to a report by Mint. The initiatives are designed to strengthen existing safeguards and introduce additional protections focused on health, safety and working conditions.
The companies stated that the expanded measures reflect a broader effort to address the challenges faced by gig workers during periods of high temperatures, with a combination of insurance coverage, emergency support and on-ground facilities.
Insurance coverage expanded
Delivery partners are now covered under an enhanced health insurance programme, which includes OPD coverage with reimbursement of up to ₹5,000, as well as IPD and day-care coverage of up to ₹1 lakh.
The plan also provides personal accident insurance with coverage of ₹10 lakh in case of accidental death.
Additionally, delivery partners and their families, including spouses and children, can access teleconsultations and online medical services, with the company bearing the full cost of premiums.
Emergency and SOS support
In case of medical emergencies, delivery partners can access ambulance services and round-the-clock SOS support through a dedicated SOS button integrated into the Zomato and Blinkit delivery partner applications.
Weather tracking and alerts
The companies stated that a weather intelligence system is being used to monitor seasonal patterns across India, enabling the delivery of real-time in-app alerts and safety guidance to help partners navigate extreme weather conditions.
Customer awareness initiatives
During periods of high temperatures, customers using the Zomato and Blinkit platforms are encouraged to support delivery partners by offering drinking water and considering tips as a gesture of appreciation.
Blinkit-specific initiatives - Rest points and hydration support
Blinkit has equipped its dark stores with summer-focused infrastructure, including pedestal fans, air coolers, seating arrangements and water dispensers. The company is also distributing more than 1.5 lakh glucose sachets daily to delivery partners.
Doctor@Store service expanded
Blinkit’s Doctor@Store initiative offers free medical consultations and treatment for delivery partners and their families. Initially launched in select locations, the service has now been expanded to cover around 50 per cent of its dark stores, with over 1.5 lakh consultations conducted so far and further expansion planned.
Zomato-specific initiatives - Nationwide rest points
Through partnerships with restaurants, Zomato has enabled access to more than 5,000 rest points across India, offering facilities such as water, shade, seating and washrooms for delivery partners.
Collaborations with TVS Motor Company and Shell have also extended rest point access to select service stations and fuel pumps, making these facilities available to delivery partners across platforms.
Cooling gear at subsidised rates
Zomato is offering cooling gear at subsidised prices through its delivery partner application, including lightweight sunscreen jackets with UPF 50+ protection aimed at reducing heat exposure during long working hours.
The initiatives highlight a growing focus on worker welfare within the gig economy, particularly as climate conditions become more extreme. As heatwaves intensify, such measures are expected to play a critical role in ensuring the safety and wellbeing of delivery partners while maintaining service continuity.
